flags.concern: Step 5's asset-panel dragover inference is still incorrect: checking `dataTransfer.types` or `hasDataTransferType('asset-kind')` only tells you that an `asset-kind` entry exists, not whether the dragged asset is `audio` or `visual`.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/AssetPanel.tsx:280-283` sets `dataTransfer.setData('asset-key', assetKey)` and `dataTransfer.setData('asset-kind', kind)`. During dragover, the types list contains the key name `asset-kind`, not its stored value. The current timeline code at `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineEditing.ts:557-560` already uses `types` only as a presence check.

flags.concern: Step 6 still does not follow the exact existing `LayoutMainContent` pattern the user called out: it keeps the editor pane on `left`/`right` positioning with `transition-[left,right]` instead of switching to the repo's `marginLeft`/`marginRight` plus `transition-[margin,padding]` approach.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx:37-50` computes `marginLeft`, `marginRight`, `paddingTop`, and `paddingBottom`, and `:63-68` applies `transition-[margin,padding] duration-300 ease-smooth`. `EditorPaneTab` still uses inline `left` and `right` at `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:124-127`, and the plan text explicitly says to add `transition-[left,right]` rather than adopt the margin-based pattern.

flags.concern: The plan body and plan metadata disagree on the render decision, which makes Step 7 ambiguous for execution and validation.
